briefdescription.para: Allocator interface for sys_hashmap.

detaileddescription.para: The Hashmap allocator can be any allocator that behaves similarly to realloc() with the additional specification that the allocator behaves like free() when new_size is zero.

parameterdescription.para: Previously allocated memory region or NULL to make a new vallocation.
the new size of the allocation, in bytes.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* sys_hashmap_iterator_t) (const struct sys_hashmap *map, struct sys_hashmap_iterator *it)
memberdef.name: sys_hashmap_iterator_t

briefdescription.para: In-place iterator constructor for sys_hashmap.

detaileddescription.para: Construct an iterator, it, for map.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* sys_hashmap_callback_t) (uint64_t key, uint64_t value, void *cookie)
memberdef.name: sys_hashmap_callback_t

briefdescription.para: Callback interface for sys_hashmap.

detaileddescription.para: This callback is used by some Hashmap methods.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* sys_hashmap_clear_t) (struct sys_hashmap *map, sys_hashmap_callback_t cb, void *cookie)
memberdef.name: sys_hashmap_clear_t

briefdescription.para: Clear all entries contained in a sys_hashmap.

simplesect.para: If the values in a particular Hashmap are

parameterdescription.para: Callback to call for each entry

memberdef.definition: typedef int(* sys_hashmap_insert_t) (struct sys_hashmap *map, uint64_t key, uint64_t value, uint64_t *old_value)
memberdef.argsstring: )(struct sys_hashmap *map, uint64_t key, uint64_t value, uint64_t *old_value)
memberdef.name: sys_hashmap_insert_t

briefdescription.para: Insert a new entry into a sys_hashmap.

detaileddescription.para: Insert a new key - value pair into map.

parameterdescription.para: Location to store the value previously associated with key or NULL
if value was inserted for an existing key, in which case old_value will contain the previous value
if a new entry was inserted for the key - value pair

memberdef.definition: typedef bool(* sys_hashmap_remove_t) (struct sys_hashmap *map, uint64_t key, uint64_t *value)
memberdef.name: sys_hashmap_remove_t

briefdescription.para: Remove an entry from a sys_hashmap.

detaileddescription.para: Erase the entry associated with key key, if one exists.

parameterdescription.para: Location to store a potential value associated with key or NULL
if map was modified as a result of this operation.
if map does not contain a value associated with key.

memberdef.definition: typedef bool(* sys_hashmap_get_t) (const struct sys_hashmap *map, uint64_t key, uint64_t *value)
memberdef.name: sys_hashmap_get_t

briefdescription.para: Get a value from a sys_hashmap.

detaileddescription.para: Look-up the uint64_t associated with key, if one exists.

parameterdescription.para: Key with which to search map
Location to store a potential value associated with key or NULL
if map contains a value associated with key.
if map does not contain a value associated with key.

briefdescription.para: Iterate over all values contained in a sys_hashmap.

parameterdescription.para: Callback to call for each entry

briefdescription.para: Clear all entries contained in a sys_hashmap.

simplesect.para: If the values in a particular Hashmap are

parameterdescription.para: Callback to call for each entry

memberdef.argsstring: (struct sys_hashmap *map, uint64_t key, uint64_t value, uint64_t *old_value)
memberdef.name: sys_hashmap_insert

briefdescription.para: Insert a new entry into a sys_hashmap.

detaileddescription.para: Insert a new key - value pair into map.

parameterdescription.para: Location to store the value previously associated with key or NULL
if value was inserted for an existing key, in which case old_value will contain the previous value
if a new entry was inserted for the key - value pair
if the size limit has been reached

briefdescription.para: Remove an entry from a sys_hashmap.

detaileddescription.para: Erase the entry associated with key key, if one exists.

parameterdescription.para: Location to store a potential value associated with key or NULL
if map was modified as a result of this operation.
if map does not contain a value associated with key.

briefdescription.para: Get a value from a sys_hashmap.

detaileddescription.para: Look-up the uint64_t associated with key, if one exists.

parameterdescription.para: Key with which to search map
Location to store a potential value associated with key or NULL
if map contains a value associated with key.
if map does not contain a value associated with key.

briefdescription.para: Check if map contains a value associated with key.

parameterdescription.para: Key with which to search map
if map contains a value associated with key.
if map does not contain a value associated with key.

briefdescription.para: Query the number of entries contained within map.

simplesect.para: the number of entries contained within map.

briefdescription.para: Check if map is empty.
Query the load factor of map.

simplesect.para: To convert the load factor to a floating-point value use sys_hash_load_factor(map) / 100.0f.
Load factor of map expressed in hundredths.

briefdescription.para: Query the number of buckets used in map.

simplesect.para: Number of buckets used in map

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct sys_hashmap *map, bool grow, size_t num_reserved, size_t *new_num_buckets)
memberdef.name: sys_hashmap_should_rehash

briefdescription.para: Decide whether the Hashmap should be resized.

detaileddescription.para: This is a simple opportunistic method that implementations can choose to use. It will grow and shrink the Hashmap by a factor of 2 when insertion / removal would exceed / fall into the specified load factor.

simplesect.para: Users should call this prior to inserting a new key-value pair and after removing a key-value pair.
The number of reserved entries is implementation-defined, but it is only considered as part of the load factor when growing the hash table.

parameterdescription.para: true if an entry is to be added. false if an entry has been removed

simplesect.para: true if the Hashmap should be rehashed
false if the Hashmap should not be rehashed

briefdescription.para: Check if a Hashmap iterator has a next entry.

simplesect.para: true if there is a next entry
false if there is no next entry

memberdef.name: SYS_HASHMAP_DEFINE_ADVANCED
memberdef.initializer: const struct _config_type _name##_config = __VA_ARGS__; \ struct _data_type _name##_data; \ struct sys_hashmap _name = { \ .api = (const struct sys_hashmap_api *)(_api), \ .config = (const struct sys_hashmap_config *)&_name##_config, \ .data = (struct sys_hashmap_data *)&_name##_data, \ .hash_func = (_hash_func), \ .alloc_func = (_alloc_func), \ }

briefdescription.para: Declare a Hashmap (advanced)

detaileddescription.para: Declare a Hashmap with control over advanced parameters.

simplesect.para: The allocator _alloc is used for allocating internal Hashmap entries and does not interact with any user-provided keys or values.

parameterdescription.para: Hash function pointer of type sys_hash_func32_t.
Allocator function pointer of type sys_hashmap_allocator_t.

memberdef.name: SYS_HASHMAP_DEFINE_STATIC_ADVANCED
memberdef.initializer: static const struct _config_type _name##_config = __VA_ARGS__; \ static struct _data_type _name##_data; \ static struct sys_hashmap _name = { \ .api = (const struct sys_hashmap_api *)(_api), \ .config = (const struct sys_hashmap_config *)&_name##_config, \ .data = (struct sys_hashmap_data *)&_name##_data, \ .hash_func = (_hash_func), \ .alloc_func = (_alloc_func), \ }

briefdescription.para: Declare a Hashmap statically (advanced)

detaileddescription.para: Declare a Hashmap statically with control over advanced parameters.

simplesect.para: The allocator _alloc is used for allocating internal Hashmap entries and does not interact with any user-provided keys or values.

parameterdescription.para: Hash function pointer of type sys_hash_func32_t.
Allocator function pointer of type sys_hashmap_allocator_t.

briefdescription.para: Declare a Hashmap.

detaileddescription.para: Declare a Hashmap with default parameters.

briefdescription.para: Declare a Hashmap statically.

detaileddescription.para: Declare a Hashmap statically with default parameters.

briefdescription.para: The default Hashmap allocator.
The default Hashmap load factor (in hundredths)
Initializer for sys_hashmap_config.

detaileddescription.para: This macro helps to initialize a structure of type sys_hashmap_config.

parameterdescription.para: Maximum load factor of expressed in hundredths

briefdescription.para: Hashmap (Hash Table) API.

detaileddescription.para: Hashmaps (a.k.a Hash Tables) sacrifice space for speed. All operations on a Hashmap (insert, delete, search) are O(1) complexity (on average).
